themes.exampleQuote: The bartenders are top class, friendly, knowledgeable about the cocktails.
Awesome, creative drinks. Bartender was excellent and friendly.
The cacio e pepe pizza is a must.
Waited 10 minutes for a sip of Jager and whiskey.
Trans folks beware, the staff didn't ask my friend's name, and instead used the deadname on their credit card.

notable_quote: Statistically, I'm bound to have less than a perfect experience here, but it hasn't happened.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers reliably praise the bartenders by name and return for the cocktail program, but service blind spots, one serious and one mundane, pull the sentiment ceiling down from where the drinks alone could take it.
quarter_narrative: Eighteen Pittsburgh TikTok creators filmed Lorelei this quarter without any coordination from the restaurant itself, and that footage ran up 1.23 million plays. @ernest alone pulled 569,300 of them on a video that never even made the food the point. The business's own TikTok account, @loreleishae3, sits in the background while the city does the work. Meanwhile Nan Xiang Soup Dumplings leads the Pittsburgh Business family this issue on the strength of social momentum, a signal Lorelei has in abundance from outside its walls but not yet from within them. The rank climbs when Lorelei's own feed starts meeting the creators already in the room.
tldr_read: Creator coverage in the millions, Instagram engagement below one percent, and a review sentiment gap the cocktail program hasn't fully closed. Ones to Watch, #2 in Pittsburgh Restaurants.
tldr_meaning: The city is already filming Lorelei at scale; the signal ceiling rises when the business's own output converts that external attention into a durable, measurable presence.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Second In Pittsburgh Restaurants, Carried By Creators. The Business's Own Feed Has Not Caught Up.

playbook.headline: Post one Reel this week, then repeat
playbook.action: Zero Reels in the last 30 days at 0.79% engagement means static posts are not moving the needle; one Reel of the cacio e pepe pizza or a bartender building a Bassett Hound changes the format entirely.

playbook.headline: Address the name-call policy publicly
playbook.action: One review about deadnaming on a credit card is sitting in a 392-review pool and pulling down sentiment; a posted policy on how orders are called closes the loop for wary diners before they read that review.

playbook.headline: Tag the creators who are already filming
playbook.action: Eighteen creators produced 1.23 million plays this quarter without a single tag or acknowledgment from @loreleipgh; a repost or story mention converts that external reach into a two-way relationship.
